title: Abbreviations of Vaccines and Other Immunizing Agents | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:
t)
HepA 
Hepatitis A vaccine (Havrix, Vaqta)
HepB 
Hepatitis B vaccine (Engerix-B,
Heplisav-B, Recombivax HB; Prehevbrio [no longer available] )
HepA-HepB 
Hepatitis A and Hepatitis B vaccine combined (Twinrix)
Hib 
Haemophilus influenzae type b conjugate vaccine (ActHIB, Hiberix, PedvaxHIB)
Hib (PRP-D) 
Haemophilus influenzae type b polyribosyl ribitol phosphate-diphtheria conjugate vaccine (Prohibit) (no longer available)
Hib (PRP-T) 
Haemophilus influenzae type b polyribosyl ribitol phosphate-tetanus toxoid conjugate vaccine (ActHIB, Hiberix) 
Hib (PRP-OMP) 
Haemophilus influenzae type b polyribosyl ribitol phosphate-outer membrane protein conjugate vaccine (PedvaxHIB) 
Hib-MenCY-TT 
Haemophilus influenzae type b-Meningococcal (bivalent) conjugate vaccine (MenHibrix)
HPV 
Human papillomavirus vaccine (Cervarix, Gardasil, Gardasil 9) 
HPV2 (or 2vHPV) 
Bivalent human papillomavirus vaccine (Cervarix)
HPV4 (or 4vHPV) 
Quadrivalent human papillomavirus vaccine (Gardasil)
HPV9 ( or 9vHPV) 
9-valent human papillomavirus vaccine (Gardasil 9) 
IPV 
Inactivated poliovirus vaccine (Ipol, Poliovax) 
IIV 
Inactivated influenza vaccine (formerly called TIV) 
IIV3 
Inactivated influenza vaccine, trivalent
IIV4 
Inactivated influenza vaccine, quadrivalent
Top of Page 
J – M 
JE-MB 
Inactivated, mouse brain-derived Japanese encephalitis vaccine (JE-Vax) (no longer available) 
JE-VC 
I
